questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy?

options.text: To sell Notion licenses directly to users
options.explanation: The Academy is a learning platform, not a sales portal.

options.text: To provide learning resources, pathways, and badge opportunities
options.explanation: The article explains that Notion Academy helps you learn Notion, earn badges, and build workflows.

options.text: To offer cloud storage for personal files
options.explanation: Cloud storage is a feature of Notion itself, not the Academy.

options.text: To host external webinars unrelated to Notion
options.explanation: Webinars are mentioned, but they are part of the Academy’s learning experience, not an unrelated service.

questions.question: What can you earn by completing a Notion learning path?

options.text: A salary raise from your employer
options.explanation: Earning a raise depends on your employer, not on completing a Notion path.

options.text: Badges that showcase your skill level
options.explanation: The article states you earn badges by completing paths.

options.text: Free additional storage space in Notion
options.explanation: Storage is not tied to badge completion.

options.text: Premium technical support for life
options.explanation: Premium support is not mentioned as a badge reward.

questions.question: Which plan type is required to become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.text: Only Enterprise plans
options.explanation: The article explicitly says any plan type qualifies.

options.text: Any plan type, regardless of company size
options.explanation: Certification is available for any plan, as stated in the text.

options.text: Only Business plans
options.explanation: Business plans are not a prerequisite.

options.text: Only Free plans
options.explanation: While free users can certify, the requirement is not limited to free plans.

questions.question: How does Notion Academy accommodate learners at different skill levels?

options.text: By offering only advanced lessons for all users
options.explanation: The Academy provides beginner, intermediate, and advanced content.

options.text: By providing distinct beginner, intermediate, and advanced learning paths
options.explanation: The article lists Essentials (Beginner), Workflows (Intermediate), and Advanced paths.

options.text: By requiring prior certification before accessing any lesson
options.explanation: Certification is optional and separate from the learning paths.

options.text: By limiting access to premium users only
options.explanation: The Academy is available to all plan types.

questions.question: Which of the following lessons is NOT listed among the featured lessons in the article?

options.text: Custom Agents
options.explanation: Custom Agents is listed as a featured lesson.

options.text: Connecting knowledge in Notion
options.explanation: This lesson appears in the featured list.

options.text: Advanced SQL Queries
options.explanation: SQL queries are not mentioned in the featured lessons.

options.text: Templates & marketplace
options.explanation: Templates & marketplace is included in the list.

questions.question: What is the typical duration of the "Building basics" lesson?

options.text: 15 minutes
options.explanation: The article states 20 minutes for this lesson.

options.text: 20 minutes
options.explanation: The "Building basics" lesson is listed as 20 min.

options.text: 30 minutes
options.explanation: 30 min is the duration for the Custom Agents lesson, not Building basics.

options.text: 40 minutes
options.explanation: 40 min matches the Templates & marketplace lesson.

questions.question: Which path includes the greatest number of individual lessons?

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: Essentials Path contains 3 lessons.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: Workflows Path contains 4 lessons.

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: Advanced Path is described as having 5 lessons, the most of the three.

options.text: All paths have the same number of lessons
options.explanation: The counts differ as shown above.

questions.question: What benefit does the Notion Certified Admin certification provide, according to the article?

options.text: Unlimited cloud storage for your workspace
options.explanation: Storage limits are unrelated to the certification.

options.text: Recognition as a certified admin and the ability to run Notion at scale
options.explanation: The article highlights that certification lets you run Notion at scale and be recognized.

options.text: Free premium support for life
options.explanation: Premium support is not listed as a certification benefit.

options.text: Automatic access to all future Notion features before release
options.explanation: Early feature access is not mentioned as part of the certification.

questions.question: Which learning path explicitly mentions the use of AI to help create connected systems?

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basics and does not reference AI.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: The article describes the Workflows Path as creating connected systems "with the help of AI".

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: Advanced Path covers automation and architecture but does not specifically cite AI in the description.

options.text: All paths equally mention AI
options.explanation: Only the Workflows Path includes an explicit AI reference.

questions.question: Based on the article, which statement best describes the relationship between "Badged Path" labels and difficulty levels?

options.text: Only Beginner‑level paths can be designated as Badged Paths
options.explanation: Both Intermediate and Advanced paths also carry the Badged Path label.

options.text: Both Beginner and Intermediate paths can be Badged Paths, but not Advanced
options.explanation: The Advanced Path is also listed as a Badged Path.

options.text: All three levels—Beginner, Intermediate, and Advanced—have Badged Paths
options.explanation: Essentials (Beginner), Workflows (Intermediate), and Advanced paths are each marked as Badged Paths.

options.text: The Badged Path label is unrelated to difficulty and assigned randomly
options.explanation: The label aligns with each defined difficulty level, not random assignment.
